title: eriogonum longifolium subsp. typicum
intro: Eriogonum longifolium subsp. typicum, also known as the mountain buckwheat or long-leaved buckwheat, is a perennial plant native to the western United States and Canada. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Eriogonum longifolium subsp. typicum include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues and promote overall well-being.
